name: 饥饿的英灵殿
short_description: A minimalist game with minimalist gameplay: go out, gather resources, return home to build, and repeat. Its drawbacks include the lack of visuals and a steep learning curve. The development team consists of just one person—the author—driven by passion and persistence.
detailed_description:
Game Introduction

Type: Text-based Sandbox Survival Game

Setting: The Realm of Valhallastarves

When the protagonist awakens, they find themselves in the Realm of the Einherjar.

As the newest guardian of this primal purgatory, your mission is to defeat Odin’s enemies and bring order to the divine realm.

Features

Creative Building, Abundant Content

Start from nothing in this sandbox survival world. Use all the resources around you to construct essential and unique facilities, such as beds, barbecue grills, chopping blocks, cauldrons, and more.

Challenging Survival, Explore the Divine Realm

Explore diverse maps, from grasslands to forests, snow-capped mountains, and plains. Collect resources, hunt various monsters, and fight to survive.

Pet Training, Relaxing Gameplay

You won’t be alone on your journey. Raise up to 261 pet birds, each with unique skills to assist you in your adventures. Feed them, enjoy their melodious songs, and relax your mind along the way.

Content You Can Experience in The Valhallastarves

• Build 20 types of functional structures.

• Upgrade buildings and research construction technologies.

• Explore 17 adventure maps and 3 dungeon maps.

• Gather various resources on the maps.

• Defeat monsters and beasts in the environment.

• Complete NPC commissions for rewards.

• Craft weapons, armor, food, and medicine from materials.

• Train 261 unique pet birds with special traits.

• Trade goods with merchants.

• Enjoy other gameplay features, with regular updates coming soon!

Friendly Reminder

▶ If you are looking for stunning visuals, this game may not meet your expectations, as it does not feature elaborate graphics.

▶ If you encounter any issues during gameplay or have suggestions and ideas, feel free to contact me anytime.
